title: Motherboards, CPUs, Firmware, TPM, Expansion, and Cooling · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic motherboards, cpus, firmware, tpm, expansion, and cooling ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in board-level compatibility and boot evidence packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate motherboard form factor, socket, chipset, buses, slots, headers, storage interfaces, ports, and replaceable components to requirements.
Compare architecture, cores, threads, clock behavior, cache, integrated features, virtualization support, power, thermals, and workload evidence.
Trace power-on, firmware settings, POST evidence, boot selection, UEFI, Secure Boot, update risk, reset, and recovery boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Board Architecture
steps.instruction:
debug case — A board and CPU change follows a failed boot and thermal throttle, but socket, firmware, lanes, power, TPM, and cooling evidence conflict. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Relate motherboard form factor, socket, chipset, buses, slots, headers, storage interfaces, ports, and replaceable components to requirements.

Inspect the Board Architecture trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Board Architecture.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate motherboard form factor, socket, chipset, buses, slots, headers, storage interfaces, ports, and replaceable components to requirements. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Board Architecture, keep Board Architecture connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Board Architecture, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

options.text: The Board Architecture record says board-level compatibility and boot evidence packet looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Board Architecture.

For inspect the failure record, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Thermal Control
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Thermal Control: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A board and CPU change follows a failed boot and thermal throttle, but socket, firmware, lanes, power, TPM, and cooling evidence conflict. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Assess heatsink contact, interface material, airflow direction, fan and pump state, dust, sensors, workload, throttling, and safe stop conditions.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Thermal Control.
steps.why: Retrieving Thermal Control through the concrete board-level compatibility and boot evidence packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Thermal Control, keep Thermal Control connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Assess heatsink contact, interface material, airflow direction, fan and pump state, dust, sensors, workload, throttling, and safe stop conditions.
